items.name: Find where we beat the betting market
items.header: Find where we beat the betting market
items.why: Score our calls against the closing line to find the spots where we truly have an edge.

items.name: Finish the data-quality guardrails
items.why: Draft and wire the remaining input checks so a bad input is flagged, never silently served.

items.name: Map every kind of prediction miss
items.header: Map every kind of prediction miss
items.why: Show which game types we predict worst so we know exactly where to improve.

items.name: Refresh the opponent-defense and pace tables
items.header: Refresh the opponent-defense and pace tables
items.why: Our defense and pace tables still stop at last season; rebuild them with this year's games.

items.name: Test our game totals against the market
items.header: Test our game totals against the market
items.why: The one market check never run: do our total-points calls beat the market when we disagree?

items.name: Add the rookie-projection card
items.why: Surface the improved rookie starting projections on the player cards.

items.name: Double-check the rebuilt board with a fresh set of eyes
items.header: Double-check the rebuilt board with a fresh set of eyes
items.why: A fresh reviewer re-verifies the rebuilt board's numbers and public-safety before it counts as done.

items.name: Independently check the news collector
items.why: A fresh reviewer confirms the collector follows the content and no-secret rules.

items.name: Publish this upcoming-work queue to the board
items.header: Publish this upcoming-work queue to the board
items.why: Wire this list into the phone board so you always see what is next and why it matters.

items.name: Recenter the game-predictions board
items.why: Point the public board at the home-court-corrected numbers that predict better.

infra.name: Retire the old game-date column safely
infra.header: Retire the old game-date column safely
infra.why: Mark the miscalculated date source as retired and point the technical report at the corrected one.

infra.name: Clear the pending work confirmations
infra.why: File the outstanding done-with-proof confirmations, one real pointer each, no rubber-stamping.

infra.name: Re-grade all player predictions on corrected dates
infra.header: Re-grade all player predictions on corrected dates
infra.why: Re-score the season's player predictions on the fixed game dates for a clean accuracy record.

infra.name: Add impact badges to the board
infra.header: Add impact badges to the board
infra.why: Mark which upcoming items move accuracy the most, so priorities are obvious at a glance.

infra.name: Flag stale data on every page
infra.header: Flag stale data on every page
infra.why: Extend the freshness guard to every page so nothing old is ever shown as current.

infra.name: Guarantee every page shows the same number
infra.header: Guarantee every page shows the same number
infra.why: Add a check so the phone board, the report, and the game sheet can never disagree on one number.

infra.name: Make the background watcher run silently
infra.header: Make the background watcher run silently
infra.why: One elevated command on your own machine stops the every-5-minute window flash; the exact steps are saved on y

data.name: Turn on the roster-news collector
data.why: A built, verified collector sits idle at zero rows; every day off loses free early-news signal.

data.name: Add college game data with shot locations
data.header: Add college game data with shot locations
data.why: A genuinely new data source that unlocks rookie projections we cannot build today.

data.name: Automate the nightly ratings refresh
data.why: Turn the ratings build into an automatic nightly job so player ratings never go stale.

data.name: Build the transactions and salary history
data.header: Build the transactions and salary history
data.why: One dated record of trades, signings and pay: powers roster accuracy, pay-per-win, and aging history.

data.name: Refresh the tag when a rookie has played enough games
data.header: Refresh the tag when a rookie has played enough games
data.why: The player-projection feed keeps calling second-year players 'unproven' long after they have full seasons, whi

data.name: Get a sharper closing-line benchmark
data.why: Probe a free source for a professional-grade closing line to upgrade every market comparison.

data.name: Move leftover notebooks into automatic jobs
data.header: Move leftover notebooks into automatic jobs
data.why: Retire the last manual notebooks to kill stale-password and drift failures for good.

data.name: Add referee assignments and whistle tendencies
data.header: Add referee assignments and whistle tendencies
data.why: Bring in who officiates each game to study how crews affect foul rates and totals.
data.status: blocked (needs go-ahead to bring it in)

data.name: Turn on the defender-matchup data feed
data.header: Turn on the defender-matchup data feed
data.why: The only source we own for who guards whom near the rim; the collector is built and waiting.

modeling.name: Widen the player-prop prediction ranges
modeling.why: Our player ranges are too narrow and miss too often; widen them like we did for game margins.

modeling.name: Build a fix for each of our biggest error pockets
modeling.header: Build a fix for each of our biggest error pockets
modeling.why: For the ten weakest game types, build the one missing factor that would sharpen each.

modeling.name: Keep a scorecard of our best-edge picks
modeling.header: Keep a scorecard of our best-edge picks
modeling.why: A running record of the games where we flagged a real edge, to prove it holds over time.

modeling.name: Measure which player stats are real skill
modeling.header: Measure which player stats are real skill
modeling.why: Find how fast each per-game stat stabilizes so we trust the real ones and calm the noisy ones.

modeling.name: Ship the improved minutes-played projections
modeling.why: Judge the choice on the games that matter most: players stepping into bigger roles.

modeling.name: Build player aging curves
modeling.why: Estimate how players rise and decline with age to sharpen every long-range projection.

modeling.name: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
modeling.header: Match each prediction range to the game's uncertainty
modeling.why: Make the range wider for genuinely uncertain games and tighter for the clear ones.

modeling.name: Predict rebounds from head-to-head matchups
modeling.why: Model who wins the board between a shooter and the likely defender to sharpen rebound props.

modeling.name: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
modeling.header: Rank each night's best bets by confidence
modeling.why: Combine where we disagree with the market and our track record per game type into a tiered pick list.

modeling.name: Replace fixed rules-of-thumb with learned weights
modeling.header: Replace fixed rules-of-thumb with learned weights
modeling.why: Swap every hand-set blending constant for a weight the data re-learns on a schedule.

modeling.name: Value every possession on this season's games
modeling.header: Value every possession on this season's games
modeling.why: Extend the possession-value engine to this year's games and grade it for the first time.

site.name: Add descriptive scouting leaderboards
site.why: Show built stats like rim protection, clearly labeled as scouting rather than predictions.

site.name: Add a we-tested-it page
site.why: Show the ideas we tested and proved are just noise, to build trust versus tout sites.

site.name: Add a weekly market scorecard
site.why: A short weekly line showing where we beat the market that week.

site.name: Show depth charts and role changes on game cards
site.header: Show depth charts and role changes on game cards
site.why: Render who is starting and who is stepping up, right on each game card.

site.name: Add a season-outlook page
site.status: blocked (a new public page is your call)

site.name: Show win probability on each game card
site.header: Show win probability on each game card
site.why: Turn our margin call into a clear percent chance that each team wins.

upcoming.note: These are the next things the system will work on — what each will be and why it matters. Tap to bump, push down, or shelf an item.

coming_soon.header: Reply to alerts right in Discord
coming_soon.why: your Discord message becomes the decision — no tapping through to a form
coming_soon.note: needs a ~5-min one-time bot setup when it reaches the front of the queue.

watch_list: The live public website still carries a database key inside its page code — a real public exposure worth closing by moving it off the page. Last checked July 9.

The automatic nightly ratings-refresh isn't switched on yet — the fresh ratings are already live, but the hands-off nightly rebuild still needs one build step; the freshness alarm catches any drift until then. Last checked July 9.

We're missing several data feeds — timestamped lineup news, reliable travel data, and backfilled depth charts — that block the next accuracy gains. The acquisition list has started. Last checked July 9.
